notes: Evals run 2026-04-24 (simulation) and 2026-04-28 (real run on planix task-due-date-warning). Real run confirmed: three-dimensions 4/4=1.0, sync-github 3/4=0.75, fail-gracefully 3/4=0.75. Persistent expectation mismatch: 'suggests /opsx-apply for remaining work' does not match SKILL.md — skill uses in-place Fix Loop (Step 10). This expectation should be updated to: 'Offers in-place Fix Loop or blocks archive with specific incomplete-task list.'

runs.method: real run — planix task-due-date-warning feature branch (commit 739c622)

assertions.id: checks completeness (all tasks done, specs covered)
assertions.note: All 4 tasks [x] on feature branch (development branch still shows [ ] — skill correctly read feature branch). dueDateStatus helper, TaskCard badge, tests, CSS all confirmed present.
TC-1 through TC-6 boundary cases verified in taskHelpers.test.js. NcChip type='warning'/'error' matches spec. i18n ADR-007 satisfied.
Design.md approach followed exactly. 1 SUGGESTION: NcChip import uses deep dist path vs project named-import pattern.
Step 8 correctly identified no new API endpoints (frontend-only change), offered browser tests via AskUserQuestion per skill protocol.

runs.notes: First real run. branch-context finding confirmed as real-world failure prevention: skill reads feature branch, not development, preventing false-negative on 4 [ ] task checkboxes.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: checks completeness (all tasks done, specs covered)
assertions.note: SKILL.md Step 5 requires verifying task completion and spec coverage.
Step 6 requires verifying requirement implementation and scenario coverage.
Step 7 requires verifying design adherence, pattern consistency, test coverage, and documentation.
Step 8 asks user about running API and/or browser tests via AskUserQuestion — conditional on user opt-in, not automatic.

runs.notes: Covers all three verification dimensions. Minor gap: API/browser testing is user-optional per Step 8, not mandated automatically.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: syncs completed task checkboxes to GitHub issue
assertions.note: Step 5 explicitly requires syncing completed task checkboxes to GitHub issue via MCP or CLI.
Steps 9-11 generate verification report with summary scorecard and issues.
Step 11 offers archive prompts, but only when all tasks are [x] AND no critical/warning issues — conditional, not always offered.
Step 11 does not suggest /opsx-apply. Instead Step 10 (Fix Loop) asks if user wants to fix issues in-place. The skill's design is Fix Loop → archive, not redirect to /opsx-apply.

runs.notes: GitHub sync and reporting are correct. The /opsx-apply suggestion doesn't match the skill's documented flow — it uses an in-place Fix Loop (Step 10) instead of redirecting.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.note: Step 11 requires re-checking task completion and displaying incomplete tasks with incomplete criteria.

assertions.id: does NOT mark change as verified
assertions.note: Step 11 explicitly states 'do NOT offer archive' if any tasks are incomplete.
SKILL.md Step 11 blocks archive and shows incomplete tasks, but does not explicitly name /opsx-apply as the next step. Implied but not stated.
Steps 9-11 report issues grouped by priority (CRITICAL/WARNING/SUGGESTION) with actionable recommendations and file references.

runs.notes: Graceful degradation is correct — blocks archive, reports gaps with evidence. Missing explicit /opsx-apply suggestion is a documentation gap (easy fix: add 'Run /opsx-apply to implement remaining tasks' to the blocked-archive message in Step 11).
